title: globularia vulgaris subsp. linnaei
intro: Globularia vulgaris subsp. linnaei, also known as the Lundy cabbage or Lundy globe daisy, is a subspecies of the Globularia vulgaris plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the bellflower family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Globularia vulgaris subsp. linnaei include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing, as well as its ability to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various conditions, including skin irritations, burns, and minor cuts and scrapes.
